question.text: A short straddle strategy is most appropriate when a trader expects which of the following?

answer.text: The underlying price will remain stable with low volatility
The underlying price will move significantly in either direction
The underlying price will increase sharply
The underlying price will decrease sharply

explanation.text: **The Logic:** A short straddle involves selling both a call and a put at the same strike. The trader collects premiums from both and profits maximally when the price stays at the strike (both expire worthless). Any significant move erodes profits. **The Trap:** A long straddle (not short) benefits from large moves. Short straddle profits from stability — the trader is effectively selling volatility. **Cross-Exam Context:** Both Series I and IV Ch5 cover short straddles in their respective markets. The logic is identical across currency, interest rate, and equity markets. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Short straddle has unlimited loss potential — use only when you are very confident that the market will remain range-bound.
